posts.name: Loren Sonkin
posts.cooked:
• 2015 Joh. Jos. Prüm Wehlener Sonnenuhr Riesling Kabinett - Germany, Mosel Saar Ruwer (12/5/2016)
    Wow. Pop and pour and I think the first glass was the best. Odd, but will this shut down? In any event buy more. It will get better. I liked this better than the 01 on release as it has no sulfur and is more elegant. Light golden in color. The nose is wonderful. There is a bit of a floral note to this. Also some pineapple and minerals. Finally, what I can best describe as a clean mountain air, maybe pine, quality. On the palate, this is slightly sweet but not cloying or obtuse. The wine has an elegance which I don’t remember noting in prior vintages. The pineapple emerges on the mid-palate, but the minerals are there all the way. Great balance. Not powerful but certainly persistent. Nice finish. Will work with or without food. Honestly, it worked great with popcorn and a bit later while smoking a cigar. An odd combination that worked well. I have a few but they are meant to age. I may need to buy a couple more for shorter term drinking. (94 pts.)

Posted from CellarTracker

posts.name: David Bueker
posts.cooked: Riesling with popcorn is almost as good as Champagne with popcorn.

posts.name: Robert Panzer
posts.cooked:
I bought a crazy amount of both the WS and GH Kab in Mag from Prüm in '15…
:0
Drank a 750 on Turkey Day, among a slew of other wines, following the wine over two days.
Classic filigree WSonnenuhr…soo good!

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked:
Happy to have fully 48 bottles of '15 Prüm, some mags too. Mainly Kab and Spät.

Prüm had to rip out a bunch of old vines in 2016 so if the quality of the vintage wasn’t enough then there’s another reason.

posts.name: Jürgen Schmidt
posts.cooked: YES, soooo good! I was stunned 3 weeks ago by the depth and openess of this Kabinett. For me too, 94/100. MUST-BUY

posts.name: Jürgen Schmidt
posts.cooked:
Here impressions of dinner chez JJ Prüm end of september.

WineBerserkers – 28 Sep 16

MOSEL - Dinner chez JJ Prüm

I had the great honor to be invited to dinner by Katharina Prüm. It took place on friday, the day of the Mosel auction. I met the legend Manfred Prüm&Amei, the eloquent husband of Katharina Prüm, the good soul of the estate Petra Pahlings, Ingrid...

posts.name: Robert Panzer
posts.cooked:
As many already know, I absolutely ADORE Prüm, and sell quite a lot of their wines.
But it does strike me as odd how many Americans worship Prüm, and then have literally NO idea about the Richter wines.
Any middle Mosel wine lover, or just wine lovers in general, owe it to check many of the Richter '15s.
Tremendous tremendous collection in '15, at craaaaazy fair prices.
Carry on with Prüm worship…
;0

I went deep on Richter as well. Only had a couple so far:

  • 2015 Weingut Max Ferd. Richter Graacher Himmelreich Riesling Kabinett - Germany, Mosel Saar Ruwer (10/30/2016)
    Wines with Friends (and Game 4) (Chris and Renee’s): My first time taking this out for a spin. All of the hype about how well Richter did in 2015, the only one I had was the base Riesling which I was not enthusiastic about. This one was much better. At $17, this is an amazing value. pop and pour, I thought it better later in the night and would give it some air. The nose has lemons and minerals but also an intriguing cherry note. Just a hint of sweetness, this seems to be more old style Kabinett which is great. Depth. 7.5% Alc. Great with food. Just a lovely wine that needs some time to develop.
    APN 2 593 049 04 16 (91 pts.)
  • 2015 Weingut Max Ferd. Richter Riesling Estate - Germany, Mosel Saar Ruwer (7/31/2016)
    A bunch of Sineann with friends (Our home): Very disappointing to me. I had heard so many good things about the 2015 vintage and while this is the low end, I had hoped for better. Light silvery yellow in color. The nose is correct but light. Wet rocks with slight lemons. On the palate, this is sweet and while not super sweet the surprising lack of acidity made it border on cloying. There is over half the bottle left, so I will revisit it later today and see. (85 pts.)

Posted from CellarTracker

posts.name: Russell Faulkner
posts.cooked: In the U.K. The Prum and Richter are pretty much the same price. 10-15% difference. I’ll take Prum most of the time

posts.name: Russell Faulkner
posts.cooked: Oh they aren’t that bad Maureen.

posts.name: Robert Panzer
posts.cooked:
Here in the US, they are not so close in price.
Maybe 50%+ higher for Prüm…
The comparison sprung to mind mind because of their presence in WS and GH, à la Prüm.
I find that when American German wine lovers hear WS, they think Prüm.
Then when I ask those people, even life long German collectors, if they have ever even tried Richter…usually “nope”.
Not like Richter’s WS Kab '15 won the Berlin Kabinett Cup or anything…(the Prüm weren’t featured in it…)
The kid is humble (30 year old son Constantin), insisting nothing has changed at this uber-traditional house (a similarity shared with Prüm), but his first vintage was '12, and I have to think that the success of '15 is not only due to Nature’s Gift that year, but his continued honing of the craft.
On a slightly different note, I am pouring the '15 Prüm WS/GH Kabs and Späts tomorrow at a '15 German tasting. I will report back with people’s impressions.
